title: Compass v0.9 signal classification accuracy
content: The 3B compass model correctly classified 101 of 105 evaluation questions into OPEN/PAUSE/WITNESS categories. Breakdown: OPEN 94%, PAUSE 94%, WITNESS 100%. Trained on 246 examples with best checkpoint at iteration 300.

title: Judge preference for compass-routed responses
content: An LLM judge evaluated compass-routed vs raw responses across 105 questions. Compass won 87 of 105 comparisons (83%). WITNESS category achieved a perfect sweep: 35 wins, 0 ties, 0 losses. The signal's value is non-uniform — it matters most where restraint matters most.

title: K-SSM order parameter at near-critical coupling
content: After fixing the gradient severance, Kuramoto oscillators initialized near critical coupling with K_ij ≈ 0.01, producing initial order parameter R = 0.3270. This is the boundary between incoherent and synchronized regimes — the oscillators begin at the phase transition.

title: Compass training data efficiency
content: The compass achieved 96% signal accuracy from only 246 training examples. Best checkpoint occurred at iteration 300. The 3B model (1.9GB) routes signals for the 9B action model (5GB, 4-bit quantized). Total system footprint: ~7GB for machine intuition.

title: Compass increases token-level entropy
content: Entropy profiling revealed that compass routing increases Shannon entropy of generated tokens. For OPEN questions: ΔH = +0.49. For PAUSE questions: ΔH = +0.45. The compass literally widens the probability field — more possibilities are considered per token under routing.

title: Routed vs raw entropy distributions
content: Mean token entropy under compass routing: H = 1.20. Mean token entropy for raw (unrouted) responses: H = 0.71. The compass doesn't just change what the model says — it changes the shape of the probability distribution from which every token is drawn.

title: Jensen-Shannon divergence between routed and raw
content: The Jensen-Shannon divergence between compass-routed and raw token distributions is JSD ≈ 0.08. This is a measurable but moderate divergence — the distributions are not different worlds, but they are different neighborhoods. Context routing shifts the distribution, it doesn't replace it.

title: IRIS framing study scale
content: The IRIS framing study executed 3,830 inference runs across multiple model architectures with factorial ablation of framing conditions. The R×E (Rigor × Empathy) interaction was the key finding — framing effects are superadditive when both dimensions are present.

title: Compass v0.8 to v0.9 improvement trajectory
content: Signal accuracy improved from 78% (v0.8) to 96% (v0.9), an 18-point gain. Judge win rate shifted from 88% to 83% — a counterintuitive decrease. Higher accuracy slightly reduced the 'happy accident' effect where wrong WITNESS signals still produced strong responses.

title: Ablation condition response timing
content: Full compass pipeline: 14.2s average per response. Raw (no compass): 10.1s. Oracle (correct signal, no reading): 11.1s. Random (wrong signal, no reading): 11.0s. The compass adds ~4 seconds of latency, almost entirely from the routing inference pass.
